headline: Agentic coding tooling led the quarter while government pressure on Anthropic escalated into a lawsuit and a model ban.
body: The dominant rotation centered on AI-assisted development and its supporting techniques: the AI layer, MCP, subagents, code navigation, the PIV Loop, and prompt caching all moved together, with concrete tooling shipping such as a Claude Code extension for Visual Studio that surfaces native diffs and shares compiler errors, plus a Cloudflare MCP configuration workflow that fell back to the API when the MCP proved insufficient. On the policy front, Anthropic faced a false advertising lawsuit over Claude Max subscription limits and a forced withdrawal of its cybersecurity models, which one account framed as politically motivated rather than a genuine jailbreak failure. Breakout entrants clustered around browser sandboxing techniques (sandbox attribute, Content Security Policy, iframe, srcdoc, origin isolation) alongside RAG, cron jobs, and predictability, the last tied to a reposted Shannon paper on the entropy of printed English. Infrastructure strain also surfaced as Microsoft turned to AWS to address a GitHub AI capacity crunch.

cited_items.title: Microsoft turns to AWS as GitHub faces AI capacity crunch
Anthropic Sued over Alleged False Advertising on Claude Max Subscription Limits
Cloudflare CAPTCHA on at least one ampersand
Show HN: Claude Code for Visual Studio (native diff with accept/reject)
Prediction and Entropy of Printed English - Claude Shannon (1950) [pdf]
The US government’s Anthropic models ban was never about an AI jailbreak
